prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(define_schema_entity primary_instance_alpha)
(define_schema_entity secondary_instance_alpha)
(define_schema_entity schema_component_user_profile)
(define_schema_entity schema_component_ordering)
(reserve_storage_backend_for_entity primary_instance_alpha storage_backend_pool_a)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(finalize_attribute_attachment_to_entity primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(reserve_shard_for_primary_instance primary_instance_alpha shard_spec_1 attribute_spec_1)
(detach_attribute_from_entity primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(assign_configurator_to_entity primary_instance_alpha configurator_agent_1)
(prepare_primary_instance_shard_allocation primary_instance_alpha shard_spec_1 configurator_agent_1)
(unassign_configurator_from_entity primary_instance_alpha configurator_agent_1)
(reserve_storage_backend_for_entity secondary_instance_alpha storage_backend_pool_b)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity secondary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(finalize_attribute_attachment_to_entity secondary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(reserve_replica_for_secondary_instance secondary_instance_alpha replica_spec_1 attribute_spec_1)
(detach_attribute_from_entity secondary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(assign_configurator_to_entity secondary_instance_alpha configurator_agent_1)
(prepare_secondary_replica_with_configurator secondary_instance_alpha replica_spec_1 configurator_agent_1)
(unassign_configurator_from_entity secondary_instance_alpha configurator_agent_1)
(create_deployment_package_from_prepared_specs primary_instance_alpha secondary_instance_alpha shard_spec_1 replica_spec_1 deployment_package_initial)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(finalize_deployment_package_before_composition deployment_package_initial primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(publish_primary_instance_with_package primary_instance_alpha deployment_package_initial)
(publish_secondary_instance_with_package secondary_instance_alpha deployment_package_initial)
(assign_version_token_to_schema_entity primary_instance_alpha version_token_v1 attribute_spec_1)
(commit_allocation_to_primary_instance primary_instance_alpha storage_backend_pool_a version_token_v1)
(detach_attribute_from_entity primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity secondary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(assign_version_token_to_schema_entity secondary_instance_alpha version_token_v1 attribute_spec_1)
(commit_allocation_to_secondary_instance secondary_instance_alpha storage_backend_pool_b version_token_v1)
(detach_attribute_from_entity secondary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(reserve_storage_backend_for_entity schema_component_user_profile storage_backend_pool_a)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity schema_component_user_profile attribute_spec_1)
(finalize_attribute_attachment_to_entity schema_component_user_profile attribute_spec_1)
(attach_data_asset_to_component_and_package schema_component_user_profile data_asset_seed_a deployment_package_initial)
(validate_component_composition_with_data_asset schema_component_user_profile data_asset_seed_a deployment_package_initial attribute_spec_1)
(attach_optimization_hint_to_component schema_component_user_profile optimization_hint_index_tuning)
(assign_configurator_to_entity schema_component_user_profile configurator_agent_1)
(apply_optimization_hint_and_prepare_component schema_component_user_profile optimization_hint_index_tuning configurator_agent_1 data_asset_seed_a deployment_package_initial)
(unassign_configurator_from_entity schema_component_user_profile configurator_agent_1)
(attach_approval_signature_to_component schema_component_user_profile approval_signature_lead)
(apply_approval_signature_and_stage_component schema_component_user_profile approval_signature_lead data_asset_seed_a deployment_package_initial)
(finalize_component_for_release schema_component_user_profile)
(assign_version_token_to_schema_entity schema_component_user_profile version_token_v1 attribute_spec_1)
(commit_allocation_to_component schema_component_user_profile storage_backend_pool_a version_token_v1)
(detach_optimization_hint_from_component schema_component_user_profile optimization_hint_index_tuning)
(remove_approval_signature_from_component schema_component_user_profile approval_signature_lead)
(detach_attribute_from_entity schema_component_user_profile attribute_spec_1)
(reserve_storage_backend_for_entity schema_component_ordering storage_backend_pool_a)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity schema_component_ordering attribute_spec_1)
(finalize_attribute_attachment_to_entity schema_component_ordering attribute_spec_1)
(attach_data_asset_to_component_and_package schema_component_ordering data_asset_seed_b deployment_package_initial)
(validate_component_composition_with_data_asset schema_component_ordering data_asset_seed_b deployment_package_initial attribute_spec_1)
(attach_optimization_hint_to_component schema_component_ordering optimization_hint_index_tuning)
(assign_configurator_to_entity schema_component_ordering configurator_agent_1)
(apply_optimization_hint_and_prepare_component schema_component_ordering optimization_hint_index_tuning configurator_agent_1 data_asset_seed_b deployment_package_initial)
(unassign_configurator_from_entity schema_component_ordering configurator_agent_1)
(attach_approval_signature_to_component schema_component_ordering approval_signature_lead)
(apply_approval_signature_and_stage_component schema_component_ordering approval_signature_lead data_asset_seed_b deployment_package_initial)
(finalize_component_for_release schema_component_ordering)
(assign_version_token_to_schema_entity schema_component_ordering version_token_v1 attribute_spec_1)
(commit_allocation_to_component schema_component_ordering storage_backend_pool_a version_token_v1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a classical planning problem in PDDL for data storage schema design. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, nothing else.


## Understanding Problem Context  
- The task involves solving a classical planning problem in PDDL for data storage schema design.  
- The goal is to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, with no additional content.

## Identifying Goal Entities  
- The goal is to commit allocations for primary_instance_alpha, secondary_instance_alpha, schema_component_user_profile, and schema_component_ordering.  
- Each requires schema_entity_allocation_committed, achieved through specific commit actions for primary/secondary instances and schema components.  

## Examining Required Actions  
- Commit actions need schema_entity_version_stamped, schema_entity_reserved_storage_backend, and version tokens, with no prior commitment.  
- Version stamping requires schema_entity_marked_released, attached attributes, and available version tokens, while backend reservation involves reserve_storage_backend_for_entity.  

## Addressing Initialization Gaps  
- None of the entities are created in the initial state, so define_schema_entity must be called for each: primary_instance_alpha, secondary_instance_alpha, schema_component_user_profile, and schema_component_ordering.  
- Storage backend reservation is needed, but with only three backends (pool_a, b, c) for four entities, planning must account for sequential reuse after commitments.  

## Considering Attribute Requirements  
- Many actions require schema_entity_attribute_attached, obtained via attach_attribute_spec, with attribute_spec_1 currently available.  
- Careful sequencing is necessary to ensure all prerequisites, like marking entities released, are met before committing allocations.

## Exploring Component Validation  
- Components require definition, backend reservation, attribute attachment, and finalization, similar to instances, but with one attribute (attribute_spec_1) needing sequential handling.  
- Validation involves finalize_component_for_release, requiring staging_complete, which comes from apply_approval_signature_and_stage_component with extension_ready and various readiness conditions.  

## Mapping Release Paths  
- For components, marking released can be achieved via finalize_component_for_release, needing staging_complete, or through composition validation like validate_component_composition_with_data_asset.  
- Instances use publish_primary_instance_with_package for release, requiring prepared status and package readiness, without needing backend assignment at publish time.  

## Planning Attribute and Backend Allocation  
- With only one attribute and three backends for four entities, actions like attach_attribute_spec and reserve_storage_backend_for_entity must be sequenced carefully.  
- Commit actions make backends and version tokens available again, allowing reuse, but requires prior steps like version token assignment and backend reservation.  

## Sequencing Instance and Component Workflows  
- Instances likely need to be prepared and published first, as package readiness is required for component data attachment and overall release.  
- Components may follow, using paths like apply_optimization_hint_and_prepare_component, ensuring composition_validated before extension_ready for further steps.

## Sequencing Entity Operations  
- Define all four entities first, including primary instances and components, to establish initial states.  
- Reserve storage backend for primary_instance_alpha using pool_a, ensuring backend_assigned and reserved status.

## Managing Attribute Attachments  
- Attach attribute_spec_1 to primary_instance_alpha, then finalize to validate, maintaining attribute_attached status.  
- Detaching attribute_spec_1 makes it available again, but validation persists, though some actions like reserve_shard still require attribute_attached.

## Handling Shard Reservations  
- Reserve shard for primary_instance_alpha using shard_spec_1, requiring attribute_attached, before detaching the attribute.  
- Preparing primary_instance_shard_allocation needs assigned configurator and shard_reserved, allowing detachment post-reservation for flexibility.

## Planning Release Steps  
- After preparation, unassign configurator to free resources, ensuring readiness for package without needing attribute attached.  
- Version token assignment and commit require attribute_attached again, necessitating careful timing of detach and reattach actions.

## Extending Release Sequences  
- After publishing primary and secondary instances, both are marked_released, with the attribute still attached to the primary.  
- Versioning the primary requires it to be released, have attribute_attached, and a reserved backend, enabling version token assignment and commit.  

## Managing Backend and Attribute Reuse  
- Commit actions make backends like pool_a available again after instance commitment, while attributes remain attached unless explicitly detached.  
- Detaching attributes from instances makes them available for reuse, such as attaching to secondary for versioning.  

## Planning Component Workflows  
- Components like user_profile and ordering require validated status and bound packages, using available data assets for attachment.  
- User_profile can leverage compliance markers like GDPR, while ordering lacks PCI, requiring alternative paths for preparation.

## Validating Component Composition
- Validating component composition for ordering with data_asset_seed_b and deployment_package_initial works, as no registered flags are set.  
- This process confirms composition_validated for schema_component_ordering, enabling further preparation steps.  

## Preparing for Extension Readiness
- Attaching optimization_hint_index_tuning to ordering and assigning configurator_1 prepares it for extension, requiring composition_validated.  
- Applying optimization and preparing the component makes it extension_ready, as it’s not replica_registered.  

## Handling Approval Limitations
- Approval_signature_lead is already attached to user_profile, making it unavailable for ordering, creating a bottleneck.  
- Removing approval from user_profile after compliance could free it for ordering, but requires careful sequencing to avoid conflicts.  

## Exploring Release Pathways
- Staging components requires approval, as all staging processes need has_approval_signature, limiting flexible release options.  
- Finalizing components for release involves staging_complete, but alternative paths like policy-based validation require further setup.

## Extending Component Preparation  
- Attaching optimization_hint_index_tuning and configurator_agent_1 to schema_component_user_profile, using data_asset_seed_a and deployment_package_initial, prepares it for extension, requiring it not be replica_registered.  
- The process results in extension_ready status, enabling further steps like approval and staging.  

## Managing Resource Reuse  
- After committing schema_component_user_profile, detaching optimization_hint_index_tuning and approval_signature_lead makes them available for reuse in schema_component_ordering.  
- Detaching resources after staging, but before finalizing the next component, ensures efficient workflow without conflicts.  

## Finalizing Component Release  
- Finalizing schema_component_user_profile for release involves staging_complete status, ensuring it’s not promotion_ready or finalized, fitting the chosen path.  
- The process includes version stamping with version_token_v1 and committing to storage_backend_pool_a, making pool_a available again.  

## Repeating Workflow for Ordering  
- The same workflow applies to schema_component_ordering, reusing pool_a, attaching data_asset_seed_b, and validating composition with attached attributes.  
- After preparing for extension and staging, ordering follows with finalization, versioning, and committing, ensuring consistency with user_profile steps.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
